shadcnTailwindV4:
shadcn CLI fully supports Tailwind v4 + React 19, and works fine on our React 18.3 (v4/React18 projects are explicitly still supported). TWO landscape shifts matter: (1) Feb 2026 collapsed the many @radix-ui/react-* packages into ONE `radix-ui` package; (2) July 2026 made Base UI (@base-ui-components/react) the DEFAULT primitive on `shadcn init` - Radix is still first-class via `shadcn init -b radix`, and every component ships for both. Pick the primitive deliberately (see risks); since we have zero primitives today it is a clean choice.

components.json for v4: set `tailwind.config: ""`, `cssVariables: true`, `tailwind.css` pointed at our styles/tailwind.css, and a `baseColor`. The CLI is CSS-first; it will try to write its canonical block.

shadcn's canonical v4 CSS (what the CLI emits) is: `@import "tailwindcss";` then `@import "tw-animate-css";` then `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *));` then raw `:root{ --background:..; --primary:.. } .dark{..}` then `@theme inline { --color-background: var(--background); ... }`. Colors are OKLCH. It dropped tailwindcss-animate for `tw-animate-css`, added `data-slot` on every primitive, and uses `size-*` over `w-*/h-*`.

Four concrete conflicts with our setup (F:\Projects\Code\Rillio\apps\web\src\styles\tailwind.css): (1) It imports the umbrella `tailwindcss` and leans on default cascade layers; we deliberately import theme.css + utilities.css UNLAYERED and skip preflight so utilities beat the legacy `*{}` reset by specificity. KEEP ours - do NOT let the CLI switch us to the umbrella import or reintroduce `@layer base`; run the CLI, then discard its layering and hand-merge tokens. (2) shadcn's `:root`/`.dark` + `@theme inline` indirection exists for light/dark runtime switching; we are single-dark with `@theme static` (static is load-bearing - v4 tree-shakes tokens the .less files reference by var() unless static). Do NOT graft the two-layer inline pattern; define shadcn color aliases directly in `@theme static` (recipe in theming). (3) You must add `@import "tw-animate-css";` (unlayered, AFTER utilities.css) so dialog/dropdown `data-[state=open]:animate-in`, fade/zoom utilities resolve - or rewrite those to our `--ease-smooth/--ease-spring`. (4) `@custom-variant dark` and every `dark:` variant in copied parts are dead weight for us (no light mode) - flatten them to base styles during the clean-room copy.
commonjsInterop:
No hard blocker. Verified stack: webpack 5.106.2 (fully exports-map aware), babel-loader + ts-loader, React 18.3.1, and clsx 2.1.1 + tailwind-merge 3.6.0 already installed. cva (class-variance-authority) is not yet present - add it; it is the shadcn variant standard, ~1kb, zero-dep, dual ESM/CJS, safe in our CommonJS-heavy tree.

Both primitive options ship dual ESM+CJS with correct `exports` maps that webpack 5 resolves cleanly: the unified `radix-ui` package (Feb 2026) and `@base-ui-components/react` (peer-supports React 17/18/19). tailwind-merge v3 is the correct line for Tailwind v4 (v2 assumed v3 utility names). Our newer TS/Tailwind files are already ESM with working interop, so shadcn .tsx parts compile through the same ts-loader/babel path - just do NOT drop them into the legacy `.js` CommonJS path.

Known gotchas to pre-empt: (a) Radix/Base UI/motion carry `"use client"` directives; webpack (non-Next) logs noisy `Module level directives cause errors when bundled, "use client" was ignored` warnings - harmless, silence with `ignoreWarnings: [/Module level directives/]`. (b) Guarantee a SINGLE React instance (`resolve.alias` react + react-dom to one copy, or pnpm dedupe) or Radix/Base UI context throws "must be used within a Provider" from a duplicated context. (c) tailwind-merge does NOT know our custom utilities (`rounded-card`, `rounded-squircle`, `size-*` tokens, semantic `--color-*`); `cn()` can silently drop or mis-dedupe them - configure `extendTailwindMerge` with class groups for our radius/color tokens, or avoid running twMerge over custom-token class strings. (d) `classnames` (legacy) and `clsx` both exist; standardize new parts on the shadcn `cn = twMerge(clsx(...))` helper. (e) react-is 18.3 is pinned - Radix Slot/composition relies on it, so keep it aligned with React.
motionLib:
Adopt the `motion` package, not `framer-motion`. In 2026 Framer Motion was consolidated INTO `motion`; you import from `motion/react` (`import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react"`). `framer-motion` still installs and re-exports for back-compat but is effectively the frozen alias - all new development and registry parts (Aceternity, Magic UI) target `motion`. Use `motion@^12` (latest 2026 line); peer supports React 18 && 19, so our 18.3.1 is fine.

For our webpack (non-RSC) shell there is no `"use client"` requirement, but the same directive-warning noise from (commonjsInterop gotcha a) applies since motion ships the directives. Bundle is ~30kb full; cut to ~15kb with `LazyMotion` + the `domAnimation` feature bundle (drops layout/drag; add `domMax` only where you need shared-layout or drag). Practical recommendation for Rillio's flat, restrained aesthetic: reach for CSS transitions + `tw-animate-css` for the common cases (pill hovers, fades, dialog enter/exit) and reserve `motion` for the few places that genuinely need gestures, scroll-linked, or shared-layout (`layoutId`) animation - this keeps most rewritten views dependency-free. Caveat: any Aceternity/Magic UI part you pull hard-depends on `motion`, so importing one commits the dep.
registryLandscape:
Ranked for Rillio (flat/borderless, one accent, data-dense), pull via `npx shadcn add @<registry>/<part>`:
1. shadcn/ui official (ui.shadcn.com) - the foundation kit: dialog, dropdown, tabs, select, tooltip, scroll-area, switch, slider. Base UI is now default (July 2026); use `-b radix` if you choose Radix. Take primitives from here first.
2. registry.directory - not a registry, the discovery/preview index for everything below; use it to browse before committing a dep.
3. OriginUI - large Tailwind-v4 set of input/button/select/segmented/pill variants; closest in spirit to our flat pill language, best source for form controls and toggles. High value.
4. Kibo UI - higher-order data components (data table, dropzone, combobox, kanban) beyond base primitives; good for the library/cached/data-heavy views.
5. tweakcn (tweakcn.com) - theme EDITOR, not components; author/tune our token->shadcn-var mapping and export Tailwind v4 CSS. Use for the theming pass.
6. Magic UI - animated components, clean shadcn-CLI compatible, but motion-heavy; cherry-pick sparingly (loaders, subtle transitions), commits you to `motion`.
7. Aceternity UI - flashy marketing/landing effects, heavy motion; mostly clashes with our restrained UI - reserve for landing page only.
8. Awesome shadcn/ui (github birobirobiro) + shadcn.io/awesome - curated meta-lists for finding niche parts.
Reality check: most third-party registries (OriginUI, Aceternity, Magic UI) still ship RADIX-based parts - that biases the primitive choice toward Radix (see risks). Treat every pulled part as a visual reference to rewrite clean-room, not a drop-in, so it inherits our tokens/icons.
risks:
1. Base-UI-vs-Radix split (highest). shadcn's default is now Base UI (July 2026), but the useful third-party registries (OriginUI, Aceternity, Magic UI) still ship Radix parts. Mixing pulls in BOTH primitive libs - duplicated deps, divergent APIs, double context. Mitigation: pick ONE and stick to it. Recommend Radix (`shadcn init -b radix`, unified `radix-ui` package) for maturity + registry compatibility; choose Base UI only if you stay strictly official. Normalize every copied part to the chosen primitive during the clean-room rewrite.
2. Tailwind-v4 layering regression. Our utilities are UNLAYERED to beat the legacy `*{}` reset by specificity, and `@theme static` is required or v4 tree-shakes tokens the .less files use (dark-on-dark). The shadcn CLI wants the umbrella `tailwindcss` import, `@layer base`, and `:root`/`.dark` + `@theme inline`. Mitigation: run the CLI for scaffolding, then discard its CSS structure; keep our unlayered theme.css/utilities.css import, define shadcn color aliases in `@theme static`, import `tw-animate-css` unlayered after utilities, never reintroduce `@layer base`.
3. tailwind-merge blind to custom tokens. `cn()`/twMerge doesn't recognize `rounded-card`, `rounded-squircle`, or our semantic `--color-*`, so it can silently drop or mis-merge them. Mitigation: `extendTailwindMerge` with class groups for our radius/color utilities, or don't twMerge custom-token strings.
4. Single-React / "use client" friction in webpack CJS. Duplicated React breaks Radix/Base UI context; directive warnings clutter builds. Mitigation: dedupe react/react-dom via resolve.alias, add `ignoreWarnings: [/Module level directives/]`.
5. Icon-set drift. Parts hard-depend on lucide-react; app standard is @stremio/stremio-icons (5.10.0). Mitigation: allow lucide as a secondary dep for shadcn-internal glyphs (select/dropdown chevrons), keep stremio-icons for product iconography, and honor the canonical bare-glyph icon-button pattern (inline-flex size-N items-center justify-center rounded-full, never padding-based).
6. Modal-as-route mismatch. shadcn Dialog/Sheet are trigger/state driven; our router is a URL view-stack where modal routes float over the beneath view. Mitigation: drive the primitive's CONTROLLED `open`/`onOpenChange` from router state (both Radix and Base UI Dialog/Popover support controlled open), render via portal over the still-visible view, and never rely on `DialogTrigger`/internal open state.
7. Motion dependency creep. Pulling one Aceternity/Magic UI part commits the whole app to `motion` (~30kb). Mitigation: default to CSS transitions + tw-animate-css; add `motion` only where gestures/shared-layout are genuinely needed, and LazyMotion+domAnimation to cap bundle at ~15kb.
